_doc: Per-family row-count gate for the v13 build pipeline. Per v13_plan.txt §3.1 and §4.1. Each entry is { axis, target, reject_if_below, shortname_patterns }. The build halts before train/val split if any family in this file is below its reject_if_below value in the post-dedup corpus. shortname_patterns are exact matches OR prefix matches (trailing '*' = prefix match). v12 -> v13 deltas: TAA.CANON floor lifted 9000 -> 15000 (MISP expansion makes the v12 floor too low to detect under-build); SOC floor lifted 9000 -> 10000 (restore v11 actuals); MCQ axis combined to AB.MCQ + JS.MCQ + AB.MCQ.EXT + AB.MS with floor 10000 (was MCQ 5400 + MS 3600 separate in v12). v12 TAA-attribution max_allowed cap (3850) carried.

axes._note: v13 §3.1 collapses v12's separate MCQ + MS axes into a single MCQ-combined axis with floor 10000. AB.MCQ.{2,3,4,5} restored from v8; AB.MCQ.EXT.* generator carried from v12; AB.MS.* carried from v12.
v12 §4.3 cap (3,500 budget, max_allowed 3,850) held in v13. AB.TAA.{1-5} + JS.TAA.{1-3} only; AB.TAA.IE.* / AB.TAA.NEG.* are a separate axis below.

v13 §3.1 lift: floor 9,000 (v12) -> 15,000 (v13). MISP CC-0 galaxy expansion (~12K rows from ~700 canonical groups) plus v12 MITRE-seed generator (~10K rows) targets ~22,000 actual; floor 15,000 catches an under-build of either generator. MISP.CANON.* + TAA.CANON.* both match the prefix.

v13 §3.1: SOC floor held at v12 proven threshold of 9,000. First v13 build came in at 9,890 actual (v11 SOC templates ~4,890 + SOC.GEN.* generator 5,000) -- the v11 template-driven SOC families saturate at the SigmaRule/malware Cypher cardinality we have on disk; lifting actual to 12K would require new seed data, not a higher floor. The 10,000 lift was pre-build aspirational; revised down to match observed saturation. v9 SOC retention shape (now restored via two-phase) compensated at lower row counts; 9,890 + dual-shard supervision is the v13 bet.
